useCases.title: e-Invoicing — FIDES Labs Small Scale Pilot
useCases.summary:
Invoice fraud, IBAN manipulation and mismatched VAT numbers cost European businesses billions every year. Today's e-invoicing is only half-digital: invoices move as PDFs or XML, but the sender's identity is never cryptographically proven.

The FIDES Labs e-Invoicing pilot — with KVK, the Belastingdienst, Unifiedpost and Visma Yuki — was delivered as three projects. In projects 1 and 3, Credenco and Sphereon worked side by side, each with their own Business Wallet, proving true cross-vendor interoperability. Project 2, the FIDES Blue Pages — a decentralised catalogue of verifiable business identity — was delivered by Credenco.

KVK and the Belastingdienst issue company credentials into the wallets, and every invoice travels wallet-to-wallet as an e-Invoice credential, cryptographically bound to the sender's KVK, VAT and IBAN. Receivers validate all three in a single check against EBSI — invoices enter the ERP pre-verified.
useCases.userJourney:
1. Onboarding — KVK issues the Chamber of Commerce number and RSIN, the Belastingdienst the VAT number and tax address, as verifiable credentials into each company's Credenco Business Wallet (OpenID4VCI). All parties are registered on the EBSI trust registry.

2. Sending — from the supplier's ERP, the invoice is passed to the Business Wallet, which wraps it in an e-Invoice credential: signed and linked to the sender's KVK, VAT and IBAN credentials, with optional evidence documents.

3. Transport — the Send Invoice API delivers the credential straight into the receiver's wallet inbox; OpenID4VP authorises the transfer. No shared mailboxes, no attachments.

4. Receiving — the receiver's wallet verifies sender identity, VAT number and IBAN against trusted issuers on EBSI. The invoice enters the ERP pre-verified, ready for straight-through processing.

5. Discovery — via FIDES Blue Pages (delivered by Credenco as project 2), organisations look up verifiable counterparty data, read from each entity's DID. In projects 1 and 3, Credenco and Sphereon Business Wallets exchanged credentials interoperably.

useCases.title: Digital Certificate of Good Conduct (VOG) as a Verifiable Credential
useCases.summary:
Every year, 1.8 million Certificates of Good Conduct (VOGs) are issued in the Netherlands. Over 350 large organizations and 26,000 sports clubs rely on the VOG to screen employees, trainers and volunteers — but the paper/PDF process is slow (1–7 weeks), manual and vulnerable to fraud.

Justis, the screening authority of the Dutch Ministry of Justice and Security, is piloting the VOG as a verifiable credential together with Credenco. Citizens receive the VOG directly in their personal wallet; employers and clubs verify it instantly through the Credenco Business Wallet. Built on open standards (W3C Verifiable Credentials, EUDI Wallet ARF, DIIP v5) for interoperability with the NL-EUDI Wallet.

Successfully piloted with sports associations: verification drops from weeks to seconds, fraud is prevented by cryptographic signatures, admin burden disappears — and Justis has a clear path to large-scale digital issuance from 2026 onward.
useCases.userJourney:
1. A citizen needs a VOG for a job or volunteer role. The requesting organization initiates the VOG application, as in the existing process.

2. Justis performs the screening and, upon approval, issues the VOG as a cryptographically signed verifiable credential via Credenco's issuance platform (OpenID4VCI).

3. The citizen receives the VOG in their personal wallet (VOG app, interoperable with DIIP v5 and NL-EUDI wallets) and keeps full control over their data.

4. The citizen shares the VOG with the relying party (OpenID4VP): remotely via an emailed validation QR code/link, or in person via a QR scan at the point of entry.

5. The relying party (employer, sports club, school) verifies the credential in real time through the Credenco Business Wallet: Justis' signature, validity and revocation status are checked automatically.

6. Onboarding proceeds immediately — no paper, no manual checks. Verification drops from 1–7 weeks to seconds.

useCases.title: Establish a New Company in One Day — Company Passport
useCases.summary:
Starting a company in the Netherlands takes about six weeks: a notary draws up the deed, KVK registers the company, the Belastingdienst issues a VAT number, and only then can a bank open a business account. Each step waits on paper from the last. Supplier onboarding is just as document-heavy: KVK extracts, tax statements, insurance and IBAN details exchanged as PDFs over email.

The Company Passport initiative — a Dutch public-private partnership of KVK, Belastingdienst, KNB (notaries), NVB and Betaalvereniging Nederland — replaces these documents with verifiable credentials issued directly by authoritative sources. Credenco delivers the sandbox, the eIDAS 2.0 / DIIP v5-compliant Business Wallet and the Issuer Portals that power the initiative.

Each authority reads the credential issued by the previous one and completes its step the same day: incorporation drops from six weeks to one day, supplier onboarding from weeks to minutes, and falsified documents become technically impossible.
useCases.userJourney:
1. The notary drafts the deed of incorporation, signs it cryptographically and issues it as a verifiable credential into the company's Credenco Business Wallet (OpenID4VCI).

2. KVK reads the deed credential, registers the company the same day and issues the KVK Registration Extract (incl. LPID and UBO data) as a credential.

3. The Belastingdienst reads the KVK extract and issues the VAT number / tax compliance credential.

4. The bank reads all three credentials, opens the business account and issues an IBAN attestation.

5. From then on, supplier onboarding is trivial: a procurement platform requests KVK, tax, VAT, IBAN and insurance credentials in a single flow (OpenID4VP). The supplier shares them in one tap from the Business Wallet, and the platform verifies the issuers' signatures in real time.

6. Result: one source document, multiple readers, instant trust — six weeks becomes one day, and onboarding checks run without PDFs, email or manual review.
